English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Forrest Lee Dimmick papers
Dimmick, Forrest Lee.
2862
7 cubic feet. 7 cubic feet.
1911-1968. Correspondence, reports, printed materials, publications, photographs. Professional papers include correspondence with Karl M. Dallenbach, Edwin G. Boring, and other participants from the Inter-Society Color Council. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 32 - mu-687 ======= 32 - mu-687 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2067387
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
I. Wald
Date completed:
1976
EAD encoding:
Michael Luther January 9, 2006
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Psychologist. Forrest Lee Dimmick, Cornell University Class of 1915, Ph.D. 1920, was a psychologist and a professor at the University of Michigan, 1921-1925. He taught at Hobart College from 1925-1947, and was head of the Vision Branch of the Naval Medical Research Laboratory, Groton, Connecticut, 1947-1963.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Professional papers include correspondence with Karl M. Dallenbach, Edwin G. Boring, and other participants from the Inter-Society Color Council concerning his research in vision, particularly color vision, night vision, visual acuity, and color aptitude testing; newsletters, color swatches, and testing materials concerning his work (1940+) with Carl Foss and other members of the Inter-Society Color Council on the development of the Color Aptitude Test (CAT), a project sponsored by the Federation of Societies for Paint Technology; correspondence, minutes and pamphlets, 1955-1967, concerning the work that Dimmick, Foss, and Deane B. Judd did as members of the Committee on Uniform Color Scales of the Optical Society of America and for the National Bureau of Standards; correspondence, notes, and reports, 1964-1967, concerning his retirement activities as a consultant for the Biological Science Communications Project; papers concerning Dimmick's color specifications research based on "Just Noticeable Differences of Hue;" papers concerning his biographical research on his former professor, Edward Bradford Titchener; letters from Titchener to Dimmick and Mrs. Dimmick; recorded interview with Harry P. Weld, a Titchener colleague, and genealogical tables of the Titchener family. Personal papers include family letters; photographs; and other material belonging to Dimmick or his wife, Claire Comstock Dimmick.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Publications written and edited by Forrest Lee Dimmick
Series II. Publications by fellow researchers covering a wide area of subjects
Series III. CAT (Color Aptitude Test) and C.I.E. (Commission International Éclairage) research items
Series IV: Inter-Society Color Council
Series V: Optical Society of America (OSA)
Series VI: Personal Activities
Series VII: Correspondence
Series VIII: Personal memorabilia
Series IX: Mrs. Claire Comstock Dimmick, Ph.D. '20
Series X: Additional material
Cite As:
Forrest Lee Dimmick Papers, #2862. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Genealogies. Psychologists. Psychology -- Study and teaching. Psychology -- Research. Psychology, Experimental. Education. Color -- Psychological aspects. Color vision. Boring, Edwin Garrigues, 1886-1968. Dallenbach, Karl M., 1887-1971. Dimmick, Claire Comstock. Foss, Carl. Judd, Deane Brewster, 1900-1972. Titchener, Edward Bradford, 1867-1927. Weld, Harry Porter, 1877-1970. Inter-Society Color Council Federation of Societies for Paint Technology Biological Science Communications Projects

July 1939 Sponsored by The American Psychology Association. Reprinted from the American Journal of Psychology.Introduction to the meeting given by F.L. Dimmick.July 1939111A symposium on Color Blindness. Arranged by the Inter-Society Color Council for the March 1943 meeting of the Optical Society of America. Includes article by F.L.D. on Methodology in Test-Preparation.March 1943111Colour Vision Conference, Cambridge 1947. comments by F.L.D. Published in Documenta Ophthalmologica. Three invited papers.August 31, 1964.111A Paper by John W. Ward given at the International Colour Meeting in Lucerne, Switzerland, June 1965. Color Instrumentation for Industry.June 1965.111A paper given at the Western Coatings Symposium, February 1966. Color Instruments take the visual Color Aptitude Test.This includes an attached letter to Dimmick relating to this paper.February 1966.111Attributes of Colour perception.Reports from international scientific discussions held in Stockholm, June 6-8, 1966. Contains a photo of Dimmick on p. 30.June 6-8, 1966.111Recent Developments in Vision Research.National Academy of Sciences. Publication 1272, Washington, D.C.,1966.111Renshaw Vision Conference, May 17-18, 1967. Institute for Research Vision, The Ohio State University. (Dimmick attended). This title contains only the program and list of attendees.May 17-18, 1967.111Report on the 16th Session of the International Commission on Illumination, June 19-28, 1967. Published by U.S. Department of Commerce. Dimmick was advisor.111List of contributors to various meetings, membership lists.111Unpublished Papers.Several versions of a paper delivered at the Eastern Psychology Association at Bryn Mawr, 1939 (applies Bridgeman's theory to psychology, i.e. Bridgeman, Philosophy of Physics.)1939112Galley proofs to The Investigation of the Olfactory Qualities.113Two versions of Color Discrimination and the Testing of Color Aptitude.Paper given 1953 at Oxford. 27 pp.114Factors in the application of the Color Aptitude Test.10 pp. with graphs and annotations. later on published in "Official Digest," December 1954, Federation of Paint and Varnish Club Production. 20 pp.December 1954115Test results, parts of the paper, An Equal Hue System of Color Specifications.116Color Specifications based on Just Noticeable Differences of Hue.Several typewritten copies, some with notes, some with annotations, graphs and test results attached.117 & 18Typewritten copy of 'Visual Perception' of Movement,with revision notes by Titchener.119Paper (typewritten and handwritten manuscript) given after 1963. No title. Subject: metamer (i.e. the same color is produced by two stimuli); another folder re metamer is in the collection.1963.120A typewritten talk with no date. Probably early. "As a psychologist..." 4 pp.121Series II. A Revision and Extension of 'The Atlas of the Munsell Color System'by A. H. Munsell Color Co., Baltimore, 1929. Abridged edition. Boxed1929.213Nagel, W. Beck Engraving Co., Philadelphia,1917.215Same as #15-revised edition.216Descriptive Color Names Dictionary.Published by Container Corporation of America.1950.217Series III. CAT (Color Aptitude Test) and C.I.E. (Commission International Éclairage) research items
Scope and Contents
In 1940 the Inter-Society Color Council began the development of its color aptitude test. At the request of professional and industrial groups, the ISCC established a subcommittee, problem 10, to develop a test that would measure an operator's capability to make valid and reliable judgemnts of the likeness and difference of color samples. Dimmick was the co-chairman of this committee and results about the findings of the subcommittee have appeared in various numbers of the newsletter.Professional tools
Scope and Contents
Color plates, slides, negatives which were used for research for CAT26 - 2" colored glass plates (some damaged). One German copy.1215Professional meetings in Sweden, 1966. (Includes correspondence.)1966.Correspondence, 1965-1967. (Includes calling cards and photos.)1965-1967.1216Meeting in Stockholm, 1966.1217Series VII: Correspondence,1929-1967Correspondence,1929-1954.1218Corresondence,1964-1965.1219Correspondence,Jan.-Dec. 1966.1220Corresondence,Feb.-Dec. 1967.1221Series VIII: Personal memorabiliaDimmick's Valedictory at Waterloo High School,June 22, 1911.1222Paper on telepathy and newspaper clipping,August 4, 1921.1223Items concerning his retirement.1224Two letters concerning his family research.1225Diplomas and certificates of merit.1226Photos.1 photo - First Co. of Psychologists, 1918. Company F Recruit Section, Hopital Battalion 14, Camp Greenleaf, Ft. Oglethorpe, Ga. Dimmick identified.1918.1311 pohoto - W. Wundt etching by Felix Pfeifer,1905.1321 photo - 'Experimentalists! Meetnig Harvard (?) probably 1922-23. Many people are identified, i.e. Weld, Boring, Dimmick, Pratt, et al.133Cornell Chapter Phi Delta Kappa, 1914-1915. Several people are identified: Dimmick, Boring1914-1915.134Photo of Conference for Visual Research Specialists. Dimmick and others are identified.1351 group photo of possibly a psychologists' meeting, c.1920.1365 unidentified photos, probably all taken at the U.S. Naval Medical Research Laboratory1373 photos which were probably taken when he received the award for his work in color research. Besides Dimmick, nobody can be identified.138Series IX: Mrs. Claire Comstock Dimmick, Ph.D. '201 letter by Titchener, Jan. 21, 1921, written to Claire Comstock.Jan. 21, 1921,12272 book reviews published in the American Journal of Psychology.12271 article (reprint) Comstock, Claire & Kittredge, Helen. An Experimental Study of children as Observers, Am. J. Psychol. April 1922, pp. 161-177.April 19221227The Book of the Class Nineteen Hundred and Twelve.Smith College, 1912. (unprocessed) ======= Series I. Publications written and edited by Forrest Lee DimmickPublications: These publications appear in chronological order, 1920-1966.Publications‡"An experimental study of visual movement and the phi-phenomenon."Am. Mostly published in the American Journal of Psychology.1925-1961Book Reviews17Notes, Discussions, Lectures, 1 galley proofNotes and Discussions18Galley Proof: A Color Aptitude Test19Lectures given c. 1937-1938.Elementary Psychology.110Symposia and CongressesSymposia and congresses (proceedings et al. of same)111A symposium on Color Tolerance. 8th Annual meeting of the Inter-Society Color Council. July 1939 Sponsored by The American Psychology Association. Reprinted from the American Journal of Psychology.Introduction to the meeting given by F.L. Dimmick.July 1939111A symposium on Color Blindness. Arranged by the Inter-Society Color Council for the March 1943 meeting of the Optical Society of America. Includes article by F.L.D. on Methodology in Test-Preparation.March 1943111Colour Vision Conference, Cambridge 1947. comments by F.L.D. Published in Documenta Ophthalmologica. Vol. III,1947.111Proceedings of the 14th International Congress of Psychology, Montreal,June 1954.111Color Symposium held at the IES national Technical Conference in Miami Beach, Florida, August 31, 1964. Three invited papers.August 31, 1964.111A Paper by John W. Ward given at the International Colour Meeting in Lucerne, Switzerland, June 1965. Color Instrumentation for Industry.June 1965.111A paper given at the Western Coatings Symposium, February 1966. Color Instruments take the visual Color Aptitude Test.This includes an attached letter to Dimmick relating to this paper.February 1966.111Attributes of Colour perception.Reports from international scientific discussions held in Stockholm, June 6-8, 1966. Contains a photo of Dimmick on p. 30.June 6-8, 1966.111Recent Developments in Vision Research.National Academy of Sciences. Publication 1272, Washington, D.C.,1966.111Renshaw Vision Conference, May 17-18, 1967. Institute for Research Vision, The Ohio State University. (Dimmick attended). This title contains only the program and list of attendees.May 17-18, 1967.111Report on the 16th Session of the International Commission on Illumination, June 19-28, 1967. Published by U.S. Department of Commerce. Dimmick was advisor.111List of contributors to various meetings, membership lists.111Unpublished Papers.Several versions of a paper delivered at the Eastern Psychology Association at Bryn Mawr, 1939 (applies Bridgeman's theory to psychology, i.e. Bridgeman, Philosophy of Physics.)1939112Galley proofs to The Investigation of the Olfactory Qualities.113Two versions of Color Discrimination and the Testing of Color Aptitude.Paper given 1953 at Oxford. 27 pp.114Factors in the application of the Color Aptitude Test.10 pp. with graphs and annotations. later on published in "Official Digest," December 1954, Federation of Paint and Varnish Club Production. 20 pp.December 1954115Test results, parts of the paper, An Equal Hue System of Color Specifications.116Color Specifications based on Just Noticeable Differences of Hue.Several typewritten copies, some with notes, some with annotations, graphs and test results attached.117 & 18Typewritten copy of 'Visual Perception' of Movement,with revision notes by Titchener.119Paper (typewritten and handwritten manuscript) given after 1963. No title. Subject: metamer (i.e. the same color is produced by two stimuli); another folder re metamer is in the collection.1963.120A typewritten talk with no date. Probably early. "As a psychologist..." 4 pp.121Series II. Publications by fellow researchers covering a wide area of subjects, i.e. psychology, color vision, night vision, color theory, 1930-1968Publications in chronological order,1930-1968.In chronological order(1930-1968)21Publications he reviewed for the Biological Sciences Communications Project.Papers reviewed for same.22Technical reports from the Department of the Navy.23MonographsHolmes, Oliver Wendell.The Stereoscope and Stereoscopic Photographs.Underwood & Underwood, New York,190324Examiner's Guide for Psychological Examining in the Army.G.P.O. Washington, 1918. (Confidential publication).1918.25The Psycholoogical Laboratory of Cornell University.Oliver B. Wood, Worcester,1900.26Butterfield, General Daniel. Address delivered on Founder's Day, January 11, 1898, Cornell University, Ithaca, N.Y.January 11, 1898,27Le Grand, Yves. Vision Lumière et éclairage.Paris, Editions de la Revue Optique.1958.28The Age of Reason, Color Systems, Color Standards: The American Coloristby Faber Birren.29Three manuals on color prepared by Martin Marietta Company.210Solving the Color Problem,published by Procelain Enamel & Manufacturing Co.211The Color Tree,published by Interchem.211Come Catch a Rainbow,published by Pittsburgh Plate Glass Company.211Color and the Use of Color,published by the Illuminating Engineering Society.211Appendix to the Questionnaire,published by the Committee on Color Terminology.211Jacobson, Egbert. The Color Harmony Manual and How to Use It.CCA, Chicago, 1948. 13 vols. Boxed.1948.212Munsell Book of Color. Defining, Explaining and Illustrating the Fundamental Characteristics of Color. A Revision and Extension of 'The Atlas of the Munsell Color System'by A. H. Munsell Color Co., Baltimore, 1929. Abridged edition. Boxed1929.213Nagel, W. Tafeln zur Untersuchung des Farbenunterscheidungs Vermoegens.Bergmann Verlag, Wiesbaden,1917.214American Optical Company. Pseudo-Isochromatic Plates for Testing Color Perception. Beck Engraving Co., Philadelphia,1917.215Same as #15-revised edition.216Descriptive Color Names Dictionary.Published by Container Corporation of America.1950.217Series III. CAT (Color Aptitude Test) and C.I.E. (Commission International Éclairage) research items
Scope and Contents
In 1940 the Inter-Society Color Council began the development of its color aptitude test. At the request of professional and industrial groups, the ISCC established a subcommittee, problem 10, to develop a test that would measure an operator's capability to make valid and reliable judgemnts of the likeness and difference of color samples. Dimmick was the co-chairman of this committee and results about the findings of the subcommittee have appeared in various numbers of the newsletter.Professional tools
Scope and Contents
Color plates, slides, negatives which were used for research for CAT26 - 2" colored glass plates (some damaged). In black case.31Black and white slides and negatives322 color negatives.336 color guides.34color swatch book35color swatch book. (NU Color scale is in card file 6)36D & H Color rule37Various color swatches used to demonstrate color standards3812" x 12" board demonstrating the gray color scale.39Vinyl color samples demonstrating color hues310, 11, 12Lakowski, R. and McDermot, A. A Bibliography on Colour.Square Publishers Ltd., Edinburgh,1966.313Flyers for various psychological and physiological testing apparati.314Bibliography on cards. (alphabetical)44Bibliography file on cards. Bruening Award for his research in the field of applied color psychology.7Series IV: Inter-Society Color CouncilNewslettersInter-Society Color Council Newsletters.
